summary: Partially decompiled `src/melee/lb/lb_00F9.c`, adding six fully matched functions and improving two more according to the decomp.dev report. The work was supported by more precise `DynamicsData`/`PolymorphicDesc` and `ColorOverlay` typing, corrected prototypes for image/DVD/GX-related helpers, and small downstream callsite updates in fighter dynamics and item collision code.
what_changed: Implemented several previously stubbed `lb_00F9` functions, including `lb_80011B74`, `lb_800121FC`, `lb_80013D68`, `lb_80014014`, and `lb_800149E0`, and improved partial matches for `lb_800100B0` and `lb_80011710`.
Changed `lb_800100B0` from a `void` prototype to returning `struct lb_80011A50_t*`, and expanded `struct lb_80011A50_t` with `x30` and `next` fields while changing `x1` to unsigned.
Reworked `DynamicsData` from a `jobj` plus opaque byte array into a typed `union PolymorphicDesc` containing variants for `lb_00F9`, fighter dynamics, hurt capsules, and absorb descriptors.
Updated `ftdynamics.c` to access fighter dynamic jobjs through `dyn_desc.data->desc.ft_unk.jobj` instead of a top-level `DynamicsData::jobj`.
Updated `itcoll.c` to access item hurt and absorb dynamic descriptor data through `desc.hurt` and `desc.absorb` rather than casting `DynamicsData` to `HurtCapsule*` or `AbsorbDesc*`.
Changed `lb_800149E0` to take an `Mtx` instead of `f32*`, and updated the item callsite in `it_266F.c` to cast `&ip->xB54` to `MtxPtr`.
Typed `ColorOverlay::x8_ptr1` as a pointer to a union supporting both `GXColor light_color` and a bitfield `light_rot`, enabling simpler matched color and light-rotation handlers.
Added a local static `GXColor yellow` symbol at `.sdata:0x804D3770` and static list heads `lb_804D63AC`/`lb_804D63B0` in `lb_00F9.static.h`.
Renamed and retyped `lbDvd_GetPreloadedArchive`'s parameter from generic `s32 arg0` to `ssize_t entry_num`, propagating that name and type through the header and implementation.
Adjusted `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` to split/retag nearby data symbols, including making some `lb_803BA1C0`/`lb_803BA1D0` data local strings and naming `.sdata:0x804D3770` as `yellow`.
smart_moves: Using a polymorphic union for `DynamicsData::desc` let existing fighter and item code become typed field accesses while preserving the observed 0x90-byte data payload size with `STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(union PolymorphicDesc) == 0x90)`.

Replacing manual `M2C_FIELD`/shift extraction in `lb_80013F78` with a `ColorOverlay::x8_ptr1->light_rot` bitfield captured the packed command format in the struct definition and simplified the matched function body.

The `lb_800121FC` implementation models the preload-or-allocate decision directly: first try `lbDvd_GetPreloadedArchive(entry_num)`, otherwise allocate an aligned texture buffer using `(buffer_size + 0x1F) & ~0x1F`.
Changing `lb_800149E0` to accept `Mtx` aligned the function body with indexed matrix accesses and required only a small, explicit `MtxPtr` cast at the known item callsite.
The symbol-table update named the local `yellow` color constant used by `GXColor4u8`, avoiding an anonymous sdata byte blob in the newly matched rendering helper.
decomp_lessons: When several systems share the same archive/dynamics payload shape, a discriminated-by-use union can be a better intermediate decomp type than repeated casts or opaque byte padding.
Packed script/command data can often be represented as a union over color structs and bitfield structs; this can remove M2C-style field extraction and help short command handlers match.
Callsite cleanup is often necessary after header fixes: making `DynamicsData` typed required updates in both fighter and item modules, not only in the library file being matched.
For GX texture setup helpers, matching may depend on exact buffer-size and alignment arithmetic as well as preload-cache fallback behavior.
Symbol scope and size corrections in `symbols.txt` can be part of matching work when newly matched functions expose that a global blob is actually a local string or local color constant.
naming_conventions: `lb_00F9` functions remain address-based names such as `lb_800121FC`, `lb_80013D68`, and `lb_800149E0` where semantic names are not yet known.
New unknown descriptor structs use module-prefixed placeholders, e.g. `lb_00F9_UnkDesc0`, `lb_00F9_UnkDesc1`, and `lb_00F9_UnkDesc1Inner`.
Union variants encode known consumers: `desc.ft_unk`, `desc.hurt`, `desc.absorb`, `desc.lb_unk0`, and `desc.lb_unk1`.
The DVD preload parameter was improved from `arg0` to `entry_num`, matching its comparison against `entry->entry_num`.
The local color constant at `.sdata:0x804D3770` was named `yellow`, reflecting its use in `GXColor4u8` calls.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use `STATIC_ASSERT` to lock down newly inferred struct and union sizes, especially `lb_00F9_UnkDesc1Inner`, `PolymorphicDesc`, and `DynamicsData`.
Represent manually shifted packed fields as signed bitfields when the generated access pattern can match the original code.
Preserve required stack padding only where still needed for matching; the diff removes `PAD_STACK(8)` from two wrapper-like functions but keeps `PAD_STACK(16)` in `lb_800149E0`.
Model recursive linked-list/DObj traversal directly: `lb_80011B74` recurses on `dobj->next` before OR-ing `dobj->mobj->rendermode`.
For matrix rendering code, type the argument as `Mtx`/`MtxPtr` so indexed expressions like `arg0[0][2]` and `arg0[1][0]` produce the desired code shape.
Use helper inlines for free-list/list-head manipulation in partially matched functions such as `lb_800100B0`, but treat the result as partial when decomp.dev still reports less than 100%.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
decomp.dev reported matched code improved by +0.03% / +1148 bytes for GALE01.

decomp.dev reported six new full matches in `main/melee/lb/lb_00F9`: `lb_80014014` (+228 bytes), `lb_800149E0` (+220), `lb_80013D68` (+212), `lb_800121FC` (+204), `lb_80011B74` (+164), and `lb_80013F78` (+32).
decomp.dev reported two improved but still unmatched functions: `lb_800100B0` at 85.71% and `lb_80011710` at 21.05%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match `lb_800100B0` or `lb_80011710` after this partial implementation.
Search for subsequent renames or semantic typing of `lb_00F9_UnkDesc0`, `lb_00F9_UnkDesc1`, and `PolymorphicDesc`.
Check whether other `DynamicsData` users were later migrated to `desc.*` union variants.
Look for later reviews or fixes around `ColorOverlay::x8_ptr1` bitfield layout and packed light rotation commands.
Search for additional uses of the local `yellow` GXColor symbol or later symbol-scope corrections near `.sdata:0x804D3770`.
